notes_md:
**cha con** = father and child (often father and son). It refers to the relationship or the pair together as a unit.

It’s a noun phrase. You often use it to talk about the two people together: *hai cha con*, *ba cha con*. You can also say *một cặp cha con* when you want to emphasize them as a pair.


In the North, people also say **bố con**; in the South, **ba con** is very common.
exampleSentences: Mối quan hệ giữa cha con rất quan trọng trong gia đình Việt Nam.
Hôm qua, cha con anh ấy cùng nhau đi câu cá ở hồ.
Bức ảnh này chụp khoảnh khắc đẹp giữa hai cha con.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The relationship between father and child is very important in Vietnamese families.
Yesterday, father and child went fishing together at the lake.
This photo captures a beautiful moment between two father and child.
notes:
cha con = father and child (often father and son). It refers to the relationship or the pair together as a unit.

It’s a noun phrase. You often use it to talk about the two people together: hai cha con, ba cha con. You can also say một cặp cha con when you want to emphasize them as a pair.

In the North, people also say bố con; in the South, ba con is very common.
